name: Wontons
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat peanut oil in a wok. Sizzle the ginger and garlic briefly, then add tofu and stir-fry for a few minutes.
2. Add scallions, sesame oil, and tamari; stir well. Set aside to cool.
3. Prepare your work area with wonton wrappers, water, and cornstarch. Place a wrapper in front of you in a diamond position.
4. Drop a heaping teaspoonful of filling into the center of the wrapper.
5. Moisten all four edges with water and pull the top corner down to the bottom, folding the wrapper over the filling to make a triangle.
6. Press the edges firmly to make a seal. Bring the left and right corners together above the filling.
7. Overlap the tips of these corners, moisten with water, and press together.
8. Place the completed wrapper on the cornstarch platter and continue until all wrappers are used.
FOR SOUP:
1. Drop wontons into boiling water and cook for 5 minutes.
2. Drain.
TO FRY:
1. Heat 2 to 3 cups of oil in a wok until hot.
2. Deep fry wontons in batches until golden, about 2 to 3 minutes on each side.
3. Drain. Serve with dipping sauce or duck sauce.
Uncooked wontons will keep in the freezer for up to 2 months if well wrapped. Thaw before frying; they can also be boiled straight from frozen and cooked for 2 minutes longer.
